summary: Added a GitHub Actions CI job and container package for running the Rust `melee-issues` tool, publishing the new `check-issues` image alongside existing CI packages, and reporting issue-check output in the GitHub step summary. The PR also adjusted existing progress-summary formatting so generated markdown is wrapped in a fenced code block.
what_changed: Created `.github/packages/check-issues/Dockerfile`, based on `rust:latest`, to build and package the `melee-issues` tool.
Created `.github/packages/check-issues/setup.sh` to install `libclang-dev`, run `cargo install --path /input/tools/issues`, and clean apt package lists.
Created `.github/packages/check-issues/entrypoint.sh` that changes to `/input` and runs `melee-issues`.

Added a `check-issues` job to `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` that pulls the `check-issues:latest` image from GHCR, mounts the repository read-only at `/input`, runs the tool, echoes the output, and appends it to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`.
Extended `.github/workflows/publish-packages.yml` so the package publish matrix includes `check-issues` in addition to `build-linux` and `gen-pages`.
Added a publish-time smoke test for the `check-issues` image by running it against the checked-out repository with `/input:ro`.
Changed the existing build progress summary in `.github/workflows/build-melee.yml` from directly appending `/tmp/output/PROGRESS.md` to wrapping its contents in a fenced code block.
smart_moves: Packaged `melee-issues` as a reusable container image instead of installing Rust dependencies during every build workflow run.
Used the same package publishing workflow matrix pattern as existing CI images, making `check-issues` part of the established GHCR package lifecycle.
Added a smoke test in `publish-packages.yml` for the new image so broken container setup is caught before relying on it in normal CI.
Mounted the repository into the runtime container read-only, which is appropriate for a checking/lint-style tool.
Normalized the computed GHCR image name to lowercase to avoid registry naming issues.
Captured tool output both in the log and in `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`, making CI issue output easier to find from the Actions UI.
decomp_lessons: Project-specific static-analysis or issue-checking tools can be made part of normal decomp CI by packaging them into a dedicated container and running them against the checked-out tree.
When a decomp workflow depends on generated diagnostic text, GitHub step summaries should format that output explicitly; fenced code blocks avoid markdown interpretation surprises.
Container setup scripts for CI tools should install only required system dependencies, install the local tool from the repo, then clean apt state to keep the image smaller.
Publishing workflow matrix entries should include a target-specific smoke test when adding a new CI container image.
naming_conventions: The new package directory and image suffix use the hyphenated name `check-issues`.
The workflow job id is `check-issues` and the visible job name is `Issues`.
The runtime entrypoint invokes the tool by its installed binary name, `melee-issues`.
The package publish workflow uses `matrix.target` values matching package directory names under `.github/packages/${{ matrix.target }}`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying `.github/packages/check-issues` or the `melee-issues` CI job.
Check whether `tools/issues` gained new checks after CI enforcement was added.
Search for failures or workflow changes involving `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` formatting.
Look for later changes from `actions/checkout@v3` or `docker/login-action@v2.1.0` upgrades.
